description: A 'molira' rises naturally above nearby land but remains locally smaller or less abrupt than a mountain. The surrounding terrain and the community describing it set the boundary, not a fixed height. 'tomora' (mountain) names the more substantial elevation.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips together for voiced 'm', sending nasal voice into middle-height rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and release along its sides into stressed high-front 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the ridge, then open into central 'a'.

semantic_domains.nature: a modest natural elevation within the surrounding terrain
semantic_domains.spatial: raised ground that can orient a route or view
